statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a 2D array `m x n` representing a matrix. Each element of the matrix is 0 or 1.
statement.outputFormat: Return a boolean indicating whether a permutation matrix can be formed by flipping the given matrix or not.
statement.notes: A permutation matrix is a square matrix that has exactly one 1 in each row and column. Note that you are allowed to flip the matrix.

constraints.description: Number of rows in the matrix
Number of columns in the matrix

hints.text: Ensure that you can flip the matrix at any position.

editorial.title: Permutation Matrix Backtracking Bug Fix
editorial.intuition: You can use a backtracking approach to check if a permutation matrix can be formed.
editorial.bruteForce: You can use a brute force approach by checking all possible configurations of 1s and 0s.
editorial.optimalSolution: Start from the top-left corner and choose a cell. Then, check if the cell can be 1 or 0. If it can be 1, move to the next cell. If it can be 0, flip the current row and column and move to the next cell.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The backtracking approach ensures that we check all possible configurations of 1s and 0s.
editorial.dryRun: Start from the top-left corner and choose a cell. Then, check if the cell can be 1 or 0.
